title: OR operators from rational coefficients
summary: Turns a rational degree-N OR coefficient system into a concrete finite ambient bidifferential sum and proves its common output weight under product and finite-sum homogeneity interfaces.

mathematicalContent.heading: Operator data
mathematicalContent.body: The datum fixes N, rational input weights and dimension parameter, and a rational coefficient C_I for every degree-N index.

mathematicalContent.heading: Ambient operator
mathematicalContent.body: Coefficients and weights are cast to ℝ before forming the finite word sum.

mathematicalContent.heading: Weight theorem
mathematicalContent.body: Every supported word has total degree N, so under the algebraic/product/sum interfaces the entire operator has weight w₁+w₂−2N.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Stores the order, two rational weights, rational dimension parameter, and degree-N rational coefficient system.

correspondence.leanType: def inputWeights (D : OROperatorData) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) : Fin 2 → ℝ
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Casts the two rational input weights to a real two-slot weight vector.
Defines the real expected output weight.

correspondence.leanType: def toOperator (D : OROperatorData) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) : CalConf.BiOperator
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Forms the finite OR-indexed ambient bidifferential operator.

correspondence.leanType: theorem toOperator_apply (D : OROperatorData) (F : CalConf.BiInput) (U : Conf.AmbientBundle) : D.toOperator CalConf F U = ∑ I ∈ ORIndex.support D.N, (D.coeffSystem.coeff I : ℝ) * I.toWord.toOperator CalConf F U
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Expands the OR operator at inputs F and ambient point U.

correspondence.leanType: theorem toWord_expectedOutputWeight (D : OROperatorData) (I : ORIndex D.N) : I.toWord.expectedOutputWeight CalConf (D.inputWeights CalConf) = D.outputWeight
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Proves that each degree-N word has the same expected output weight as the OR datum.

correspondence.leanType: theorem mapsWeights (D : OROperatorData) (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (Hprod : CalConf.ProductHomogeneityIdentities) (Hsum : PolyLaplacianCombinationData.OperatorSumHomogeneityIdentities CalConf) : CalConf.MapsWeights (D.toOperator CalConf) (D.inputWeights CalConf) D.outputWeight
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Under algebraic, product-homogeneity, and finite-sum homogeneity interfaces, proves the operator's weight mapping.
Builds a word-indexed shadow datum by imaging the OR support and summing coefficients of indices that represent the same word.

references.locator: Classification of invariant bilinear differential operators
references.note: Flat-model origin of the coefficient family.
Primary curved ambient source for the encoded OR recurrence.

limitations: The output-weight theorem depends on three abstract homogeneity interfaces. The word-indexed shadow is constructed, but this file does not prove equality between its operator and the preferred OR-indexed sum, nor any defect cancellation or tangentiality.
